title: quercus affinis var. typica
intro: Quercus affinis var. typica is a plant species known as Himalayan or Eastern Oak, a type of deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and bark, which contain flavonoids and tannins with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus affinis var. typica include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness their medicinal properties.
